data.title: BETH KRAMER JOINS CULINARY INSTITUTE OF AMERICA
data.description: The Culinary Institute of America (CIA) based in Hyde Park, N.Y., disclosed on March 19, 2026, that Beth Kramer has been appointed as the Vice President overseeing Development and Alumni Relations. A seasoned fundraising executive, Kramer brings her expertise to the CIA to drive initiatives and strengthen connections with alumni. This strategic move underscores the institution's commitment to advancing philanthropic activities and engaging with its alumni community.

data.title: Atlas Free and the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime Convene East African Anti-Trafficking Summit in Kenya
data.description: On March 19, 2026, in Mombasa, Kenya, Atlas Free collaborated with the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) to organize a regional summit focused on combating trafficking. The summit convened leaders from Ethiopia, Kenya, and various other nations to address this crucial issue. The event underscored the significance of global cooperation in addressing challenges related to trafficking.

data.title: Office of Strategic Capital Founder Dr. Jason Rathje joins Elroy Air’s Defense Advisory Board
data.description: Elroy Air, a prominent U.S.-based developer of autonomous aerial systems for middle-mile logistics, has revealed that Dr. Jason Rathje is now a member of the company’s Defense Advisory Board. Rathje, who previously served as the founding director of the Department of War’s Office of Strategic Capital (OSC), led efforts to garner private investment in crucial technologies essential for U.S. national security. Under Rathje's leadership, OSC was able to oversee a credit authority totaling up to $200 billion.

data.title: Electric Utilities Must Strengthen Compliance Governance to Safeguard Grid Reliability, Advises Info-Tech Research Group
data.description: As electric utilities rapidly modernize their grid systems, compliance governance is becoming increasingly intricate. Recent findings from Info-Tech Research Group reveal that utilities must establish structured oversight in both their IT and operational technology sectors to avoid potential audit shortcomings. Failure to do so could have serious consequences for utilities operating in this evolving landscape.

data.title: Dino Rizzo Joins Global Church Leaders at Lead to Win Leaders Gathering 2026
data.description: Dino Rizzo recently participated in the Lead to Win 2026 conference, where he shared valuable leadership perspectives on resilience, maintaining healthy souls, and long-term faithfulness. The event took place in KELLER, Texas on March 18, 2026, and brought together global pastors, worship leaders, and church officials. The conference featured discussions on vital topics for leaders within the church community and attracted a significant number of attendees.
